summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class provides a read-only view of the job that is provided to the tasks while they are running.

CORE LOGIC:

The class provides methods to get information about the job, such as the job name, the number of reduce tasks, the job ID, the working directory, the input format class, the mapper class, the combiner class, the reducer class, the output format class, the partitioner class, the raw comparator used to compare keys, the user-specified job name, the user-defined raw comparator for grouping keys of inputs to the combiner, the user-defined raw comparator for grouping keys of inputs to the reduce, the job-setup and job-cleanup needed for the job, the task-cleanup needed for the job, the task profiling enabled, the profiler configuration arguments, the range of maps or reduces to profile, the reported username for the job, the symlink, the cache archives set in the configuration, the cache files set in the configuration, the localized caches, the localized files, the file entries in classpath as an array of Path, the archive entries in classpath as an array of Path, the timestamps of the archives, the timestamps of the files, the configured number of maximum attempts that will be made to run a map task, and the configured number of maximum attempts that will be made to run a reduce task.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The class does not have any inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:

The class depends on the following classes: Configuration, Credentials, JobID, Path, Class, InputFormat, Mapper, Reducer, OutputFormat, Partitioner, RawComparator, IntegerRanges, URI, IOException.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This file contains the definition of MRJobConfig, a Java interface that defines configuration keys for the MapReduce framework. It also includes configuration keys for memory and CPU requirements for the mapper and reducer, as well as keys for the MRAppMaster and a Hadoop MapReduce job.

CORE LOGIC:

The file does not contain any specific algorithms or processing patterns.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The file does not specify any data flowing in or out.

DEPENDENCIES:

The file does not import, parent, or collaborator classes.

You may obtain a copy of the License at * * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 * * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and * limitations under the License. */ package org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce; import org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience; import org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceStability; import org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configurable; /** * Partitions the key space. * *

Partitioner controls the partitioning of the keys of the * intermediate map-outputs. The key (or a subset of the key) is used to derive * the partition, typically by a hash function. The total number of partitions * is the same as the number of reduce tasks for the job. Hence this controls * which of the m reduce tasks the intermediate key (and hence the * record) is sent for reduction.

* *

Note: A Partitioner is created only when there are multiple * reducers.

* *

Note: If you require your Partitioner class to obtain the Job's * configuration object, implement the {@link Configurable} interface.

* * @see Reducer */ @InterfaceAudience.Public @InterfaceStability.Stable public abstract

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class implements a mapper/reducer class that can be used to perform field selections in a manner similar to unix cut.

CORE LOGIC:
The input data is treated as fields separated by a user specified separator (the default value is "\t"). The user can specify a list of fields that form the map output keys, and a list of fields that form the map output values. If the inputformat is TextInputFormat, the mapper will ignore the key to the map function. and the fields are from the value only. Otherwise, the fields are the union of those from the key and those from the value.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The field separator is under attribute "mapreduce.fieldsel.data.field.separator".
The map output field list spec is under attribute "mapreduce.fieldsel.map.output.key.value.fields.spec".
The value is expected to be like "keyFieldsSpec:valueFieldsSpec".
key/valueFieldsSpec are comma (,) separated field spec: fieldSpec,fieldSpec,fieldSpec ...
Each field spec can be a simple number (e.g. 5) specifying a specific field, or a range (like 2-5) to specify a range of fields, or an open range (like 3-) specifying all the fields starting from field 3. The open range field spec applies value fields only. They have no effect on the key fields.

Here is an example: "4,3,0,1:6,5,1-3,7-". It specifies to use fields 4,3,0 and 1 for keys, and use fields 6,5,1,2,3,7 and above for values.

The reduce output field list spec is under attribute "mapreduce.fieldsel.reduce.output.key.value.fields.spec".

The reducer extracts output key/value pairs in a similar manner, except that the key is never ignored.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.io.Text class and the org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience and org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceStability classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class treats a line in the input as a key/value pair separated by a separator character.

CORE LOGIC:

The separator can be specified in the config file under the attribute name mapreduce.input.keyvaluelinerecordreader.key.value.separator. The default separator is the tab character ('\t').

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The input is a line in the input. The output is a key/value pair.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the LineRecordReader class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- This class is an InputFormat for reading plain text files.
- It breaks the files into lines and divides each line into key and value parts.
- If no separator byte exists, the key will be the entire line and the value will be empty.

CORE LOGIC:
- The createRecordReader method creates a KeyValueLineRecordReader for reading the input split.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The input is a generic InputSplit and the output is a KeyValueLineRecordReader.

DEPENDENCIES:
- This class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.input package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The NLineInputFormat class is a FileInputFormat that splits N lines of input as one split.

CORE LOGIC:

The getSplits method splits the input files into splits based on the number of lines per split.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The createRecordReader method returns a LineRecordReader, which reads and consumes at least one character out of its upper split boundary.

DEPENDENCIES:

The NLineInputFormat class depends on the LineRecordReader class, which is used to read and consume the input data.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: Extracts text matching a regular expression.
CORE LOGIC: Uses a regular expression pattern to match and extract text from input.
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: Expects input key and value of type K and Text, respectively. Emits Text keys and LongWritable values.
DEPENDENCIES: Depends on the Configuration class for setting up the regular expression pattern and group.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The BinaryPartitioner class is a custom partitioner for use with Apache Hadoop MapReduce. It is used to partition BinaryComparable keys based on a configurable subset of the bytes array returned by the getBytes() method.

CORE LOGIC:
The BinaryPartitioner class uses the left and right offsets specified in the configuration to determine the subset of bytes to be used for partitioning. It then computes a hash of this subset and uses it to determine the partition.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The BinaryPartitioner class takes a BinaryComparable key, a value, and the number of partitions as input, and returns the partition index as output.

DEPENDENCIES:
The BinaryPartitioner class implements the Configurable interface, which allows it to access configuration properties. It also uses the WritableComparator class from the Apache Hadoop framework to compute the hash of the subset of bytes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The KeyFieldBasedComparator class is a WritableComparator that provides a subset of the features provided by the Unix/GNU Sort. It supports sorting numerically and reversing the result of comparison.

CORE LOGIC:
The compare method compares two byte sequences based on the key specifications provided. It uses the KeyFieldHelper class to parse the key specifications and extract the relevant fields from the byte sequences. It then compares the fields based on the specified options.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The compare method takes two byte sequences as input and returns an integer value indicating their relative order.

DEPENDENCIES:
The KeyFieldBasedComparator class depends on the KeyFieldHelper class for parsing the key specifications and extracting the relevant fields from the byte sequences. It also depends on the WritableComparator class for comparing the byte sequences.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The KeyFieldBasedPartitioner class is a custom partitioner for Apache Hadoop MapReduce. It is used to partition keys based on certain key fields, as specified by the user.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of the KeyFieldBasedPartitioner class is implemented in the getPartition() method. This method takes a key, a value, and the number of reduce tasks as input, and returns the partition number for the given key.

The method first checks if there are any key fields specified by the user. If not, it simply returns the partition number based on the hash code of the key.

If key fields are specified, the method converts the key to a byte array and calculates the start and end indices of each key field within the byte array. It then computes a hash code based on the key fields and returns the partition number based on this hash code.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The KeyFieldBasedPartitioner class takes a key, a value, and the number of reduce tasks as input, and returns the partition number for the given key.

DEPENDENCIES:

The KeyFieldBasedPartitioner class depends on the KeyFieldHelper class, which is used to parse and process the key field specifications. It also depends on the Configuration class, which is used to retrieve the job configuration.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- Parse key specifications of the form -k pos1[,pos2], where pos is of the form f[.c][opts], where f is the number of the field to use, and c is the number of the first character from the beginning of the field.
- Set key field separator.
- Get word lengths from a given string.
- Get start and end offsets for a given key specification.
- Parse key options.

CORE LOGIC:
- Parse key specifications and set key field separator.
- Get word lengths from a given string.
- Get start and end offsets for a given key specification.
- Parse key options.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- Set key field separator: input is a string representing the key field separator.
- Get word lengths: input is a byte array, start index, and end index. Output is an array of integers representing the lengths of the words in the string.
- Get start and end offsets: input is a byte array, start index, end index, array of word lengths, and a KeyDescription object. Output is the start and end offsets for the given key specification.
- Parse key options: input is a string representing the key options.

DEPENDENCIES:
- org.apache.hadoop.util.UTF8ByteArrayUtils
- java.util.List
- java.util.ArrayList
- java.util.StringTokenizer
- java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The RehashPartitioner class is a custom partitioner for Apache Hadoop MapReduce.
- It rehashes the hash code of the key object to determine the partition for the key-value pair.
- This can help to distribute the data more evenly across partitions, potentially improving reduce time.

CORE LOGIC:
- The getPartition() method takes the key, value, and the number of reduce tasks as input.
- It first applies a bitwise XOR operation with a prime number seed to the hash code of the key.
- Then, it applies additional bitwise operations to improve the hash code's quality.
- Finally, it takes the resulting hash code modulo the number of reduce tasks to determine the partition.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The getPartition() method takes the key, value, and the number of reduce tasks as input.
- It returns the calculated partition number.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The RehashPartitioner class is part of the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.partition package.
- It implements the Partitioner interface, which requires the implementation of the getPartition() method.

DEPENDENCIES: None.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
TTConfig is a placeholder for MapReduce server-level configuration.

CORE LOGIC:
TTConfig defines the following constants:
- TT_INDEX_CACHE: specifies the size of the shuffle index cache in megabytes
- TT_MAP_SLOTS: specifies the maximum number of map tasks that can run on the task tracker
- TT_RESOURCE_CALCULATOR_PLUGIN: specifies the resource calculator plugin to be used by the task tracker

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
TTConfig does not define any inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
TTConfig imports the following classes:
- MRConfig: defines common configuration constants for MapReduce
- InterfaceAudience: defines the audience of the interface
- InterfaceStability: defines the stability of the interface

This chunk of code loads deprecated keys and values from a properties file.
The code uses a DeprecationDelta class to store the mapping between the old and new keys.

The code depends on the Configuration and DeprecationDelta classes.

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code contains utility methods for MR Job configuration.

CORE LOGIC:
The code includes methods for redacting job configuration properties, setting task progress report intervals, and setting the frequency of logging of task attempts.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a Configuration object as input and modifies it in-place.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library and theslf4j logging library.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

Destroy the process-tree.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this chunk is to destroy the process-tree. It does this by sending a specified signal to the specified pid.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this chunk are the pid of the root process of the subtree of processes to be killed, the time to wait before sending SIGKILL after sending SIGTERM, and whether the pid is a process group leader or not. The output is the destruction of the process-tree.

DEPENDENCIES:

This chunk depends on the org.apache.hadoop.util.Shell.ShellCommandExecutor class.
